description: Labeled prompt set for CRG-RIS discrimination testing. Labels are the expected ordinal tier based on Promptest's stated rubric (Specificity, Clarity, Scope, Verification, Calibration), constructed independently of the judge so they serve as ground truth.

prompts.text: build me a website like Instagram
Can you fix the bug in my login function? It's not working right.
Add a rate limiter to my Express API so people can't spam it.
Refactor this React component to be cleaner and add error handling, also maybe add tests and improve performance and fix any bugs you see and update the styling too.

In src/login.ts (TypeScript), the login() function throws instead of returning a 401 response when the password is wrong. Fix it so wrong-password attempts return { status: 401, body: { error: "invalid_credentials" } }. Don't change the unknown-user path.
Add a sliding-window rate limiter middleware to my Express API, keyed by IP, 100 requests per 60 seconds. Use an in-memory store, no Redis. Return 429 on limit exceeded.

In src/api/rateLimiter.ts (Node/TypeScript, Express middleware), add a sliding-window rate limiter keyed by req.ip that allows 100 requests per 60-second window. Use an in-memory Map<string, number[]> of timestamps — no Redis, this is single-instance for now. On limit exceeded, respond 429 with JSON { error: "rate_limited", retryAfterMs: <number> }. Don't touch the existing auth middleware in the same file. Add a unit test in src/api/rateLimiter.test.ts covering: under-limit passes through, over-limit returns 429, and the window correctly slides (old timestamps expire). Run pnpm test rateLimiter when done and paste the output.

In src/login.ts (TypeScript), the login() function currently throws for invalid passwords instead of returning a 401. Root cause: compareSecret()'s rejection on a mismatch isn't caught, so it propagates up instead of being treated as a normal invalid-credentials case. Fix: catch the compareSecret rejection (or otherwise handle the mismatch) and return { status: 401, body: { error: "invalid_credentials" } } — do not change the unknown-user path (email not found should still return the same 401 shape it does today). Verify your fix by running: pnpm exec tsx hidden_test.ts — it should print allPass: true and exit 0.
